compiled_state.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can invoke 3-tier SC coverage agents with prompt files defining input/output contracts per tier
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create 3 agent prompt files (haiku, sonnet, opus) for SC coverage evaluation with explicit per-tier input/output contracts.
**Why**: The sprint gate step cannot dispatch coverage agents until well-defined prompt files exist with unambiguous I/O schemas. Without these contracts, S2a and S2b would be building against implicit assumptions about data shape and serialization.
**Scope**:
- IN: haiku prompt file (citation-required: must name specific covering child ID or escalate), sonnet prompt file (independent evaluation, no escalation context, returns COVERED/MISSING/UNSURE per SC), opus prompt file (COVERED-preferred tie-breaking for intent-ambiguous SCs, returns COVERED or MISSING only), input schema definition per tier, output JSON schema definition per tier
- OUT: Sprint SKILL.md gate step integration (S2a), test suite (S2b), documentation updates (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a haiku prompt file exists that requires the agent to cite a specific covering child ID for each COVERED verdict; absence of a citation means mandatory escalation
  ← Satisfies: "Haiku confirms only SCs it can validate by citing a specific covering child"
- When this story is complete, a sonnet prompt file exists that instructs independent evaluation without escalation context, returning COVERED/MISSING/UNSURE per SC
  ← Satisfies: "Sonnet evaluates each escalated SC independently"
- When this story is complete, an opus prompt file exists with COVERED-preferred tie-breaking for intent-ambiguous SCs, returning only COVERED or MISSING
  ← Satisfies: "Opus is the final arbiter…defaults to COVERED when intent is plausible"
- When this story is complete, each prompt file defines the expected input format (SC list + children descriptions structure) that the sprint gate step must supply
  ← Satisfies: "Dedicated prompt files defining input schema"
- When this story is complete, each prompt file specifies the exact JSON output schema (field names, types, envelope) that the sprint gate step will parse
  ← Satisfies: "Dedicated prompt files defining output schema"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] 3 new prompt files increase maintenance surface area; consider a shared base template with tier-specific overrides to reduce duplication across haiku/sonnet/opus contracts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
